meta.title: Grid
meta.description: The Grid Layout Component provides a flexible and responsive way to structure content using a two-dimensional grid system. It allows elements to be arranged in rows and columns, enabling dynamic and efficient layouts for different screen sizes. The component accepts all the chakra supported properties and Grid.Item as children elements.

mdx:
## Basic usage

Use Grid → When you need fine control over layout, spanning, and custom grid
behaviors.

```jsx
import { Grid } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
```

To use the Grid layout, ensure to wrap the individual grid items inside of the
`<Grid.Item>` component

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Grid gap="100">
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 1
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 2
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
)
```

### With columns

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Grid gap="100" templateColumns="repeat(2, 1fr)">
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 1
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 2
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
)
```

### With varying layout

Showcases how elements can span multiple rows and columns within a structured
grid. This layout is useful for dashboards, content cards, or complex UI
components where elements need different sizes while maintaining alignment.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Grid
    h="200px"
    templateRows="repeat(2, 1fr)"
    templateColumns="repeat(5, 1fr)"
    gap={4}
  >
    <Grid.Item rowSpan={2} colSpan={1}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        colSpan=1
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={2}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        colSpan=2
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={2}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        colSpan=2
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={4}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        colSpan=4
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
)
```

### With template areas properties

Demonstrate layout with template areas - shows how to create a common page
layout pattern

```jsx live
const App = () => (
 <Grid
    h="400px"
    templateAreas={`
      "header header"
      "nav main"
      "footer footer"
    `}
    templateColumns="200px 1fr"
    gap="400"
  >
    <Grid.Item gridArea="header">
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full">
        Header
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item gridArea="nav">
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full">
        Navigation
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item gridArea="main">
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full">
        Main Content
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item gridArea="footer">
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full">
        Footer
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
)
```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Grid templateColumns="repeat(3, 1fr)" gap="400">
    <Box bg="gray.100" p="400">Item 1</Box>
    <Box bg="gray.100" p="400">Item 2</Box>
    <Box bg="gray.100" p="400">Item 3</Box>
  </Grid>
);
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Use semantic HTML:** Grid is a layout component that renders a `div` by
  default. Use the `as` prop to apply semantic elements when appropriate.
- **Maintain reading order:** Ensure the visual grid order matches the DOM order
  so screen readers navigate content logically.
- **Avoid layout-only containers:** When possible, apply grid styles directly to
  semantic elements rather than wrapping content in non-semantic divs.
- **Responsive design:** Ensure grid layouts adapt appropriately for different
  screen sizes and zoom levels.
- **Focus order:** Ensure keyboard focus follows a logical order through grid
  items.
- **Don't rely on visual position alone:** Important relationships or
  information should be conveyed through semantic HTML, not just visual
  positioning.

### Resources

- [WCAG 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/meaningful-sequence.html)
- [WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/focus-order.html)

dev.mdx:
## Getting started

### Import

```tsx
import { Grid, type GridProps } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
```

### Basic usage

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Grid gap="100">
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 1
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 2
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 3
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
);
```

## Usage examples

### Column-based layout

Use the `templateColumns` prop to define the number and size of columns in your grid.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Grid gap="100" templateColumns="repeat(3, 1fr)">
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 1
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 2
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 3
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 4
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
);
```

### Row and column spanning

Grid items can span multiple rows or columns using the `rowSpan` and `colSpan` props on `Grid.Item`.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Grid
    h="200px"
    templateRows="repeat(2, 1fr)"
    templateColumns="repeat(5, 1fr)"
    gap="100"
  >
    <Grid.Item rowSpan={2} colSpan={1}>
      <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" h="full">
        Sidebar
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={2}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full">
        Top Left
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={2}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full">
        Top Right
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={4}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full">
        Bottom Section
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
);
```

### Template areas

Use named grid areas to create semantic layouts. This approach is particularly useful for common page layouts.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Grid
    h="400px"
    templateAreas={`
      "header header"
      "nav main"
      "footer footer"
    `}
    templateColumns="200px 1fr"
    gap="400"
  >
    <Grid.Item gridArea="header">
      <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" h="full">
        Header
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item gridArea="nav">
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full">
        Navigation
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item gridArea="main">
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full">
        Main Content
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item gridArea="footer">
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full">
        Footer
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
);
```

### Responsive grids

Grid properties can be responsive using Chakra UI's responsive syntax with breakpoint objects.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Grid
    gap="100"
    templateColumns={{
      base: "1fr",
      sm: "repeat(2, 1fr)",
      md: "repeat(3, 1fr)",
      lg: "repeat(4, 1fr)",
    }}
  >
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 1
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 2
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 3
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 4
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
);
```

### Gap variations

Control spacing between grid items using the `gap` prop, or use `columnGap` and `rowGap` for independent control.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="400">
    <Box>
      <Text mb="100" fontWeight="semibold">
        Uniform gap
      </Text>
      <Grid gap="200" templateColumns="repeat(3, 1fr)">
        <Grid.Item>
          <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
            Item 1
          </Box>
        </Grid.Item>
        <Grid.Item>
          <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
            Item 2
          </Box>
        </Grid.Item>
        <Grid.Item>
          <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
            Item 3
          </Box>
        </Grid.Item>
      </Grid>
    </Box>

    <Box>
      <Text mb="100" fontWeight="semibold">
        Different row and column gaps
      </Text>
      <Grid rowGap="400" columnGap="100" templateColumns="repeat(3, 1fr)">
        <Grid.Item>
          <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
            Item 1
          </Box>
        </Grid.Item>
        <Grid.Item>
          <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
            Item 2
          </Box>
        </Grid.Item>
        <Grid.Item>
          <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
            Item 3
          </Box>
        </Grid.Item>
        <Grid.Item>
          <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
            Item 4
          </Box>
        </Grid.Item>
        <Grid.Item>
          <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
            Item 5
          </Box>
        </Grid.Item>
        <Grid.Item>
          <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7">
            Item 6
          </Box>
        </Grid.Item>
      </Grid>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
);
```

## Component requirements

### Usage context

**Grid vs SimpleGrid**: Use Grid when you need fine-grained control over layout with template areas, custom spanning, or complex grid configurations. Use SimpleGrid for simpler, uniform grid layouts where items have equal size.

**Grid.Item wrapper**: Always wrap child elements in `Grid.Item` components to ensure proper grid behavior and enable spanning props.

## Accessibility

**Role**: Grid renders as a `<div>` with `display: grid`. It has no implicit ARIA role.

**Labeling**: For grids that represent data tables or require context, use `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`:

```tsx
<Grid aria-label="Product catalog">
  {/* Grid items */}
</Grid>
```

**Keyboard navigation**: Grid itself doesn't provide keyboard navigation. If your grid contains interactive elements, ensure they maintain logical tab order.

### Persistent ID

If your use case requires tracking and analytics for this component, it is good practice to add a **persistent**, **unique** id to the component:

```tsx
const PERSISTENT_ID = "product-grid";

export const ProductGrid = () => (
  <Grid id={PERSISTENT_ID} templateColumns="repeat(3, 1fr)" gap="400">
    <Grid.Item>
      <ProductCard />
    </Grid.Item>
    {/* More items */}
  </Grid>
);
```

## API reference

<PropsTable id="Grid" />

## Common patterns

### Dashboard layout

A responsive dashboard layout with varying widget sizes.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Grid
    templateColumns="repeat(12, 1fr)"
    templateRows="repeat(3, 100px)"
    gap="400"
  >
    <Grid.Item colSpan={{ base: 12, md: 8 }} rowSpan={2}>
      <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" h="full" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="200">
          Main Chart
        </Text>
        <Text color="neutral.11">Primary analytics visualization</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={{ base: 12, md: 4 }} rowSpan={2}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="200">
          Statistics
        </Text>
        <Text color="neutral.11">Key metrics summary</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={{ base: 12, md: 4 }}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold">Recent Activity</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={{ base: 12, md: 4 }}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold">Notifications</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={{ base: 12, md: 4 }}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold">Quick Actions</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
);
```

### Card gallery with featured item

A gallery layout where one item is featured more prominently.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Grid
    templateColumns="repeat(4, 1fr)"
    templateRows="repeat(2, 150px)"
    gap="400"
  >
    <Grid.Item colSpan={2} rowSpan={2}>
      <Box
        p="400"
        bg="primary.7"
        h="full"
        borderRadius="md"
        display="flex"
        flexDirection="column"
        justifyContent="center"
        alignItems="center"
      >
        <Text fontWeight="bold" fontSize="xl" mb="200">
          Featured Product
        </Text>
        <Text color="neutral.11">Premium collection highlight</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={2}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold">Product 2</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold">Product 3</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" h="full" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold">Product 4</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
);
```

### Sidebar layout with sticky navigation

A page layout with a sticky sidebar using template areas.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Grid
    minH="400px"
    templateAreas={`
      "sidebar content"
    `}
    templateColumns="250px 1fr"
    gap="400"
  >
    <Grid.Item gridArea="sidebar">
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" borderRadius="md" position="sticky" top="0">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="300">
          Navigation
        </Text>
        <Stack gap="200">
          <Text>Dashboard</Text>
          <Text>Products</Text>
          <Text>Orders</Text>
          <Text>Customers</Text>
          <Text>Settings</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item gridArea="content">
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" borderRadius="md" minH="400px">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="300">
          Main Content
        </Text>
        <Text color="neutral.11">
          Your page content goes here. The sidebar will remain visible as users
          scroll.
        </Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
);
```

### Form sections grid

Organize form fields in a structured grid layout.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Grid templateColumns="repeat(2, 1fr)" gap="400">
    <Grid.Item colSpan={2}>
      <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold">Account Information</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="200">
          First Name
        </Text>
        <Text fontSize="sm" color="neutral.11">
          Input field placeholder
        </Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="200">
          Last Name
        </Text>
        <Text fontSize="sm" color="neutral.11">
          Input field placeholder
        </Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={2}>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="200">
          Email Address
        </Text>
        <Text fontSize="sm" color="neutral.11">
          Input field placeholder
        </Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item colSpan={2}>
      <Box p="400" bg="primary.7" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold">Contact Preferences</Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="200">
          Phone
        </Text>
        <Text fontSize="sm" color="neutral.11">
          Input field placeholder
        </Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box p="400" bg="neutral.7" borderRadius="md">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="200">
          Preferred Contact Method
        </Text>
        <Text fontSize="sm" color="neutral.11">
          Select placeholder
        </Text>
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
  </Grid>
);
```
